title: Race-year grouped validation and OOF training
summary: Random row splits can leak near-identical race/lap/stint context across train and validation.
rationale: Random row splits can leak near-identical race/lap/stint context across train and validation. Public sources on grouped CV recommend non-overlapping groups, and F1 strategy backtests are naturally race/session based. A race-year or race-only holdout may reduce misleading local AUC and reveal features that generalize to unseen races.
implementation_hint: Replace the single random train_test_split with StratifiedGroupKFold using groups such as Year + '_' + Race, or Race if Year is sparse. Use out-of-fold predictions for validation and then refit on full train for submission. Compare current random AUC with grouped AUC before trusting leaderboard moves.
expected_effect: May not immediately raise random CV, but should improve public/private robustness and prevent selection of leakage-prone row-neighbor features.
risk: If Kaggle test contains same races as train, grouped validation may be too conservative and reject useful transductive race-level features.
